claim_statement: A coordinated-compromise assessment must compute posterior odds from calibrated or bounded likelihoods and an explicit loss matrix before recommending escalation or de-escalation.

local_model.name: Bayesian security decision model
local_model.scope: coordinated-compromise hypothesis assessment against benign alternatives

boundary_or_interface.statement: Escalate when posterior odds exceed LFP/LFN; if denominators are missing, the assessment is incomplete.
limits: does not endorse attribution without discriminating evidence

non_claims: Does not assert that any specific system is compromised.
Does not permit default escalation from capability presence alone.

validation_gates.expected: security_decision present with non-missing denominator discipline

promotion_criteria: Provide same-population baseline telemetry or explicitly bounded denominator ranges.
Register discriminating evidence before adversarial attribution.
known_limits: Likelihood-ratio computation depends on reference data and remains uncertain when baselines are unavailable.
